arts.ttl: Nicolas Jackson denied as Chelsea held to goalless draw against Everton
arts.sum: The Friedkin Group’s era at Everton started as Farhad Moshiri’s tenure ended, with a goalless draw against a London club with designs on the top of the Premier League, and a strengthening of Sean Dyche’s case for the stabilisation job that the new owners require. Chelsea missed the chance to go top of the Premier League as their eight-match winning run in all competitions came to a halt at Goodison Park. Enzo Maresca’s insistence that Chelsea lack the experience and pedigree for a sustained title challenge was given credence as his players toiled against a resolute Everton defence. Dyche’s side had the clearer opportunities and, despite failing to take any, departed the more satisfied team at the end of a momentous few days for the club. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca must prepare for more frustrating tests as Chelsea run out of ideas against Everton
arts.sum: Chelsea’s flying start to season will lead to sides showing them more respect

arts.ttl: Chelsea miss chance to go top with Everton draw
arts.sum: Chelsea were denied the chance to go top of the Premier League table as they were held to a 0-0 draw by Everton at a blustery Goodison Park on Sunday.

arts.ttl: 'It's not fair': Pat Nevin felt really sorry for £39m Chelsea player during Everton match
